name: Rancher Kubernetes Management API
what_it_does: Open-source Kubernetes multi-cluster management platform with REST API — enabling DevOps teams to provision, manage, and monitor multiple Kubernetes clusters across any infrastructure (on-premises, AWS, GCP, Azure, bare metal) with centralized access control, RBAC, monitoring integration, and a developer-friendly dashboard, with REST API for automation and GitOps workflows.
use_cases: Provisioning Kubernetes clusters via Rancher API for infrastructure automation
Managing multi-cluster deployments via Rancher API for centralized Kubernetes operations
Implementing RBAC across Kubernetes clusters via Rancher API for enterprise access control
Deploying Helm charts via Rancher API for application lifecycle management
Integrating Rancher with CI/CD pipelines via API for GitOps-driven Kubernetes deployments
not_for: Single Kubernetes cluster on managed cloud (use EKS/GKE/AKS directly for single cluster)
Teams without Kubernetes expertise (Rancher requires K8s knowledge)
Serverless/PaaS use cases where Kubernetes complexity is unwanted
best_when: An enterprise team managing multiple Kubernetes clusters across hybrid/multi-cloud environments needs centralized control — where Rancher provides unified management, RBAC, monitoring, and automation across all clusters via API.
avoid_when: You're running a single managed Kubernetes cluster, don't have Kubernetes expertise, or need PaaS-level abstraction over containers.

auth.notes: API key (Bearer token) authentication. API keys created in Rancher UI (Account & API Keys). HTTP Bearer token in Authorization header. Keys scoped to specific clusters or global. No OAuth2 as primary auth — LDAP/Active Directory/GitHub/SAML for user auth integrated. No fine-grained API key scopes (permissions via RBAC on user role). No webhooks — event-driven via Rancher Webhook Admission Controller. SUSE Rancher (formerly Rancher Labs, acquired by SUSE 2020).

pricing.paid_tiers: SUSE Rancher Prime: commercial support from SUSE (subscription pricing); Rancher Government Solutions
pricing.notes: Created by Rancher Labs (Sheng Liang, Shannon Williams). Acquired by SUSE 2020 (~$600M). Apache 2.0 license. GitHub: rancher/rancher (23,000+ stars). Go-based. Kubernetes management: provision clusters on AWS EKS, GKE, AKE, custom; deploy Helm apps; RBAC; Projects (namespace grouping); Rancher Desktop (local K8s for developers). RKE (Rancher Kubernetes Engine) — custom K8s distro. K3s — lightweight K8s (also by Rancher/SUSE). Fleet (GitOps for multi-cluster). Monitoring via Prometheus/Grafana integration. Used by: automotive, defense, manufacturing, healthcare enterprise. SUSE offers commercial support through SUSE Rancher Prime.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted — no rate limits; server capacity dependent

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with Bearer token. Kubernetes-native API (kubectl also works). Apache 2.0 open source. SUSE-backed. Self-hosted. RBAC controls. No webhooks. User auth via LDAP/GitHub/SAML — API keys for automation. Multi-cluster management focus.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Cluster reads idempotent by cluster ID. Workload reads idempotent. Cluster provisioning: check state before provisioning to avoid duplicates. Kubernetes declarative model is inherently idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Rancher API is a superset of Kubernetes API — can also use kubectl directly for cluster operations
API keys scoped to Rancher user permissions — RBAC controls what the key can do
Cluster ID format: c-xxxxx — distinct from Kubernetes cluster name; use Rancher API to list cluster IDs
No webhooks — use Kubernetes Admission Webhooks or Rancher Fleet for event-driven automation
SUSE acquisition 2020 — Rancher is now SUSE Rancher; commercial support via SUSE subscription
RKE1 vs RKE2 vs K3s — three different Rancher-ecosystem K8s distros with different APIs

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. RBAC at cluster/namespace/project level. Bearer token auth. User auth: LDAP/AD/GitHub/SAML (enterprise identity). Apache 2.0 open source — auditable. Self-hosted — data never leaves your infrastructure. Audit logs built-in. SUSE commercial support adds enterprise security review. CIS Kubernetes benchmark compliance scanning built-in.
